query_text: What are the conditions a Nigerian court must satisfy before it can assume jurisdiction to hear a matter?
notes: Core jurisdiction competence test. All four SC cases discuss want of jurisdiction and conditions precedent to hearing.

query_text: On what grounds can a suit be dismissed for want of jurisdiction in the Federal High Court?
notes: FHC-specific jurisdiction. Williams v Adold/Stamm is a commercial case where jurisdiction was contested.

query_text: What is the test for striking out a statement of claim as disclosing no reasonable cause of action?
notes: Striking out pleadings — no reasonable cause of action. All cases discuss the threshold test under Nigerian procedure rules.

query_text: What factors must a court consider when deciding whether to grant an interlocutory injunction to preserve the status quo pending the determination of a suit?
notes: American Cyanamid-equivalent Nigerian test for interlocutory injunction. All five cases directly apply the balance of convenience / serious question to be tried test.

query_text: When will a Nigerian court refuse to grant an injunction to restrain a party from dealing with its assets pending trial?
notes: Negative limb of injunction — when refused. Zenith Bank, Nika Fishing, and Adetona all discuss refusal of injunctive relief.

query_text: What are the principles governing an application for stay of execution of a judgment pending appeal in Nigeria?
notes: Stay of execution is a key motion type. All five cases deal with the principles: arguable grounds of appeal, nugatory appeal, balance of hardship.

query_text: Under what circumstances will a court grant a stay of execution where the judgment sum is capable of being recovered if the appeal succeeds?
notes: Focuses on the nugatory/recoverability branch of the stay of execution test.

query_text: What is the doctrine of locus standi and what must a plaintiff demonstrate to establish standing to sue in a Nigerian court?
notes: Locus standi — a basis for challenging suit at the outset. Adegbite, Adetona, Maranroola and Umanah all discuss standing requirements.

query_text: How does the principle of res judicata operate to bar a fresh action in Nigerian courts where the same parties litigated the same issues before?
notes: Res judicata as a ground for striking out or dismissing. All four SC cases directly apply the doctrine.

query_text: What are the requirements of natural justice and the right to fair hearing under section 36 of the Constitution of Nigeria?
notes: Section 36 CFRN fair hearing. Garba v Unimaid, FCSC v Laoye, Falomo v Lagos State are landmark fair hearing SC cases.

query_text: When will a Nigerian court strike out a suit for being statute-barred under the applicable limitation law?
notes: Limitation period as a ground to strike out. Kolawole v Pezzanni is a key limitation of actions SC case.

query_text: What is the effect of a breach of contract and what remedies are available to the innocent party under Nigerian law?
notes: Breach of contract and remedies — often background for injunction or stay applications in commercial disputes.

query_text: What is the distinction between a preliminary objection and a motion on notice, and when should each be used to challenge the competence of a suit?
notes: Procedural route for challenging competence. All four cases involve preliminary objections or challenges to court competence.

query_text: What principles govern the award of costs in Nigerian civil proceedings and when will a successful party be deprived of costs?
notes: Costs principles — relevant in many motion contexts. Okafor v Abumofuani, Chitra Knitting, and New Nigeria Newspapers all address costs.

query_text: How does a court exercise its inherent jurisdiction to prevent abuse of its process, and when will it strike out proceedings as an abuse of process?
notes: Abuse of process / inherent jurisdiction — important strike-out ground. Adegbite v Amosun, Aminu v Hassan, and Esuwoye v Bosere all apply this doctrine.

query_text: What must a claimant prove to establish trespass to land and obtain an order for possession in Nigeria?
notes: Trespass to land — proof of title and possession. Key SC cases from 2008-2017.

query_text: On what grounds can an election petition be struck out for non-compliance with the Electoral Act in Nigeria?
notes: Election petition competence — pre-hearing strike out on Electoral Act grounds.

query_text: What is the test for granting bail pending appeal in a criminal matter before a Nigerian court?
notes: Bail pending criminal appeal — analogous to stay of execution test adapted for criminal proceedings.

query_text: What elements must be established to succeed in a claim for defamation under Nigerian law?
notes: Defamation elements — publication, identification, damage to reputation. SC cases from 1990.

query_text: What is the procedure for enforcing a judgment debt against a judgment debtor who refuses to pay in Nigeria?
notes: Judgment enforcement — writ of execution, garnishee proceedings. Macaulay v NAL Merchant Bank is key.

query_text: How does a court determine whether to grant or refuse an application to enforce fundamental rights under the Fundamental Rights (Enforcement Procedure) Rules?
notes: Fundamental rights enforcement procedure — standing, mode of commencement, reliefs available.

query_text: When will a Nigerian appellate court interfere with a finding of fact made by the trial court?
notes: Appellate interference with findings of fact — perverse findings, evaluation of evidence.

query_text: What are the conditions precedent that must be fulfilled before filing an action against the government or a public authority in Nigeria?
notes: Pre-action notice requirement for suits against government — Public Officers Protection Act, state notice laws.

query_text: How does a court determine whether a contract is void for uncertainty or lack of essential terms under Nigerian law?
notes: Contract formation — certainty of terms, agreement on essential terms. Adetoun Oladeji v Nigerian Breweries is key.

query_text: What principles govern the grant of a Mareva injunction (freezing order) to prevent a defendant from dissipating assets before judgment?
notes: Mareva injunction — good arguable case, real risk of dissipation, balance of convenience.

query_text: What is the effect of non-service or defective service of originating process on the jurisdiction of a Nigerian court?
notes: Service of process and court jurisdiction — defective service vitiates proceedings, motion to set aside.

query_text: Under what circumstances will a court grant an order of specific performance of a contract in Nigeria?
notes: Specific performance — adequacy of damages, readiness and willingness, unique subject matter.

query_text: What are the rights of a mortgagor whose property has been sold by a mortgagee exercising power of sale under Nigerian law?
notes: Mortgagee power of sale — duties owed to mortgagor, injunction to restrain sale, accounting.

query_text: What is the Court of Appeal's jurisdiction to hear interlocutory appeals in civil matters without leave?
notes: CoA appellate jurisdiction — interlocutory vs final decisions, right of appeal vs leave requirement.

query_text: What is the principle of nemo dat quod non habet and how does it apply to disputes over title to property in Nigeria?
notes: Nemo dat — root of title, bona fide purchaser for value, competing claims to land.
